content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Nancy V. Dooley 
Dooley & Leibovitz 
1255 Post Street, suite 437 
San Francisco, CA 
94109 
Dear Ms. Dooley: 
June 28, 1989 
Re: Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-352 
You have requested advice on behalf of the San Francisco Of-
ficial Court Reporters Association Political Fund (SFOCRA) regard-
ing campaign disclosure obligations under the Political Reform Act 
(the "Act") .1./ 
QUESTION 
Is the SFOCRA required to file reports as a "county general 
purpose recipient committee" or as a "state general purpose 
recipient committee"? 
CONCLUSION 
The SFOCRA is a "county general purpose recipient committee" 
for purposes of when and where to file its campaign disclosure 
reports. 
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory references 
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission 
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations section 
18000, et ~ 
All references to regulations are to Title 2, 
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660 


Nancy V. Dooley 
Page 2 
FACTS 
The SFOCRA has filed campaign statements as a state general 
purpose recipient committee since it filed its first campaign 
statement for the period 1/1/76 through 1/6/77. 
The SFOCRA makes 
direct contributions or expenditures to support or oppose only 
local candidates or measures being voted upon in the city and 
county of San Francisco. 
In addition, the committee also makes 
monetary contributions to the Political Action California Court 
Reporters Association, a state general recipient purpose commit-
tee. From the inception of SFOCRA in 1976, it has made five 
separate contributions totaling $3,780, to the state committee. 
During the same period, contributions to support local candidates 
or measures totaled $12,680. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 82027.5(c) defines a "county general purpose 
recipient committee" as a "committee to support or oppose 
candidates or measures voted on in only one county, or in more 
than one jurisdiction within one county." 
With regard to where campaign statements are filed, Section 
84215(d) states in part that " ••• county general purpose recipient 
committees shall file the original and one copy with the clerk of 
the county ••• " 
Section 82027.5(b) defines a "state general purpose recipient 
committee" as a "committee to support or oppose candidates or 
measures voted on in a state election, or in more than one 
county." 
However, a committee active primarily within a single city or 
a single county, which makes occasional contributions to state 
candidates and state general purpose recipient committees or state 
measures, would not meet the definition of a state general purpose 
recipient committee. 
(Whitaker Advice Letter, No. A-87-115, copy 
enclosed.) 
If the committee begins to regularly make contribu-
tions to candidates for state office or to state general purpose 
recipient committees, or becomes involved to any significant 
degree in state elections, it should begin filing as a state 
general purpose recipient committee. 


Nancy V. Dooley 
Page 3 
It appears the SFOCRA should have indicated on the statement 
of organization (Form 410) that it was a county general purpose 
recipient committee, and thereafter, filed its campaign statements 
with the San Francisco Registrar of Voters. 
The committee should 
amend its original statement of organization to reflect its status 
as a county committee rather than a state committee. 
If you have any questions regarding this letter, please 
contact me at (916) 322-5662. 
cc: Secretary of State 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
t J 
By: 
Sandra Taylor 
Political Reform Consultant 


LAW OFFICES 
NANCY J. dg;PLE1 
tl 06 fll fB9 
PAULA C. LEIBOVITZ 
DOOLEY & LEI savITz 
1255 POST STREET, SUITE 437 
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94109 
TELEPHONE: (4151 B85-400S 
June 5, 1989 
Sandra Taylor, Campaign Consultant 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J Street, 7th Floor 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Re: 
San Francisco Official Court Reporters 
Association Political Fund, I,D. 1770681 
Dear Ms. Taylor: 
BERKELEY OFFICE 
2000 CENTER STREET 
P. O. BOX 1029 
BERKELEY, CALIFORNIA 94701 
TELEPHONE: {4151 845-2823 
REPLY TO: 
SAN FRANCISCO 
The SFOCRA Political Fund is seeking a determination from 
the Fair Political Practices Commission that it is properly a 
local General Purpose Recipient Committee instead of a State 
General Purpose Recipient Committee. 
The San Francisco Official Court Reporters Association 
Political Fund (hereafter ·SFOCRA Political Fund") has filed 
Campaign Statements as a State General Purpose Recipient 
Committee since it filed its first Committee Campaign Statement 
for the period 1-7-76 through 1-6-77. 
As I told you when we spoke yesterday, the SFOCRA Political 
Fund makes direct contributions or expenditures to support or 
oppose only local candidates or measures being voted upon in the 
City and County of San Francisco. 
In addition to local 
contributions or expenditures the SFOCRA Political Fund also 
makes monetary contributions to the Political Action California 
Court Reporters Association, a State General Purpose Recipient 
Committee. 
From the inception of the SFOCRA Political Fund in 
1976 five separate contributions have been made to the State PAC 
totaling $3,780. 
During the same period contributions to 
support local candidates or measures totaled $12,680. 
We look forward to your determination that the SFOCRA 
Political Fund is alaI General Purpose Recipient Committee. 
, 
NJD:mc 
~ cc: 
Harvey Tsuboi - Office of Secretary of State 
Carl Sauceda - Treasurer, SFOCRA Political Fund

REPLY TO: 
SAN FRANCISCO 
to 
SFOCRA 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Nancy V. 
Dooley 
Dooley & Leibovitz 
June 8, 1989 
1255 Post street, Suite 437 
San Francisco, CA 
94109 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-352 
Dear Ms. Dooley: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on June 7, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
{See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
JP:plh 
eanne Pritchard 
Chief Technical Assistance 
and Analysis Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804..0807 • (916) 322 .. 5660
